overall_summary: ElizaOS is transitioning from an open-source framework into a commercial SaaS ecosystem with the introduction of elizacloud, Jeju blockchain, and the xProof decision provenance plugin. Significant infrastructure consolidation is underway to streamline the DegenAI hosting environment into a single Spartan instance.
key_facts: Odilitime confirmed ElizaOS and DegenAI are the only two tokens officially minted and supported by the labs.
The xProof.app plugin was released to anchor agent decisions on the MultiversX blockchain for immutable provenance.
Infrastructure consolidation is shifting production and development environments into a single staging Spartan instance.
The AI16Z token (Jeju) is designated to power an upcoming native blockchain rather than serving as a memecoin.
A partnership was announced with Nosana for a Builders' Challenge starting March 25 with workshops on March 26 and April 2.
The Pythia MCP server was launched allowing agents to query Chainlink oracles on Polygon without API keys.
BitDelta exchange offered a free listing for AI16Z following internal due diligence.
A supply chain attack on the litellm-pypi package was identified as a security risk for the community.
open_questions: Anyone here built any ai agents that are trading autonomously?
What is the relationship between bas milady and sol milady.ai? Does the milady app support both of them?
Will milady join this (Nosana Builders' Challenge)?

twitter_news_highlights.claim: Solana Foundation CPO predicts 99.99% of on-chain transactions will be driven by agents and bots within two years.
Vitalik Buterin and Beff Jezos debated E/ACC vs D/ACC, focusing on crypto as a trust layer for AI.

new_issues_prs.title: xProof plugin in ElizaOS plugin registry
new_issues_prs.author: jasonxkensei
new_issues_prs.significance: Provides on-chain decision provenance for agents

new_issues_prs.title: Major database refactor to improve long-term system architecture
new_issues_prs.author: unknown

overall_focus.claim: The framework is prioritizing decentralized identity via the SAID Protocol and expanding reach through WhatsApp and Gmail integrations.

discord_updates.summary: Clarifications on the framework vs. SaaS business model (elizacloud). Detailed discussions on token utility for Jeju and DegenAI's role as a platform host.
Promotion of hatcher.host for ElizaOS/Milady hosting and technical deep-dive into xProof.app for MultiversX integration.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users who missed the AI16Z migration window are reporting limited recourse, with only a waitlist currently available.
Positive response to the new Pythia MCP server for eliminating the need for private API keys in DeFi agent operations.

strategic_insights.insight: The shift toward elizacloud indicates a maturing business model that separates the free open-source framework from premium hosting and deployment services.
implications_or_questions: Will the core framework maintenance slow down in favor of SaaS features?
How will competing hosting providers like hatcher.host impact the elizacloud rollout?

strategic_insights.insight: The introduction of xProof and SAID Protocol suggests a strong move toward verifiable and legally/technically accountable AI agents.
implications_or_questions: Is this a preparation for stricter regulation of autonomous trading agents?

market_analysis.observation: Eliza token performance shows a pattern of 20-30% relief pumps followed by dumps to new all-time lows.
market_analysis.relevance: Reflects low investor confidence or high sell pressure despite technical progress.
